page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores built on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation with single-threaded cores and no hyperthreading. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gb.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in Passmark memory testing. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth are also strong, reaching 296.88 GB/sec and 138.33 GB/sec respectively. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via Geekbench are rated strong, while stress-ng CPU benchmarks fall into the average tier. The instance shows strong performance in database operations, single-threaded gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. However, it exhibits weaker performance in LLM text generation for small models and prompt processing for medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based architecture offers a resource-dense profile with high memory bandwidth relative to standard x86 configurations, making it a viable option for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory without the premium of specialized hardware. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and text processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with no hyperthreading, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and single-core Geekbench scores. Conversely, it shows average CPU stress-test results and weaker performance in certain LLM inference tasks. This hardware profile is highly suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose application workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.4xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 33 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 33 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64-powered general-purpose instance featuring dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and balanced resources for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory benchmark performance
33 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators
Strong performance in database operations and data decompression tasks
